title: Look Up: Remarks at Duke University Economics Department Commencement
summary: A commencement address built around the theme of 'look up' with admiration, curiosity, optimism and teamwork, drawing on Logan's career at the New York Fed (9/11, the 2008 crisis, the March 2020 pandemic turmoil). It is inspirational and ceremonial. Only a single closing paragraph touches the macro backdrop, noting a Middle East energy shock, AI transforming jobs, a balanced-but-slow-to-hire labor market and inflation 'still too high,' but it offers no stance read or policy-path signal, so it is non-policy for reaction-function purposes.

key_quotes.quote: Although the labor market is in balance, many employers are slow to hire.
key_quotes.context: The one macro aside in an otherwise ceremonial commencement speech.
Brief backdrop comment to graduates, not tied to any policy recommendation.
